arabic_text: يَعْلَمُ مَا يَلِجُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا وَمَا يَنزِلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَاءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلرَّحِيمُ ٱلْغَفُورُ
english_text: He knows what enters into the earth and what comes out of it, and what comes down from the sky and what rises to it, and He is the constantly merciful, exceedingly forgiving.
alt_text: Quran verse decomposed phrase-by-phrase with colour-coded English glosses under each Arabic chunk: 'يَعْلَمُ' (He knows), 'مَا يَلِجُ فِي الْأَرْضِ' (what enters into the earth), 'وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا' (and what comes out of it), 'وَمَا يَنْزِلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ' (what comes down from the sky), 'وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا' (and what rises to it), 'وَهُوَ الرَّحِيمُ الْغَفُورُ' (constantly merciful, exceedingly forgiving).
notes: Quran 34:2 (Sura Saba'). Used to illustrate the symmetric/chiastic ordering inside a single verse — earth/sky and entry/exit pairings.
